title: Puri (Deep-Fried Bread)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Whole wheat flour (atta) 2 cups (250g) Use fine quality chapati atta
Semolina (sooji / rava) 1 tbsp Optional – adds crispness
Salt ½ tsp Adjust to taste
Oil or ghee 1 tbsp For kneading
Oil For deep frying Use neutral oil (like sunflower oil)

instruction.title: Dough Preparation
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine atta, semolina, and salt.
Add 1 tbsp oil/ghee and rub into the flour.
Gradually add water and knead into a firm, non-sticky dough.
Tip: Puri dough should be tighter than roti dough to avoid absorbing oil.
Cover and rest the dough for 15–20 minutes.

instruction.title: Rolling the Puri
steps: Divide the dough into 20 equal portions and roll into balls.
Lightly grease your rolling surface – do not use dry flour (it burns in oil).
Roll each ball into a small disc (4–5 inches diameter), evenly thick.

instruction.title: Deep Frying
steps: Heat oil in a deep kadhai/wok to 180–190°C (355–375°F).
Check with a tiny dough piece – it should rise immediately and sizzle.
Gently slide a puri into the hot oil.
Press lightly with a slotted spoon – it will puff up.
Flip and fry until light golden on both sides (takes ~30 sec per puri).
Remove onto a paper towel to drain excess oil.

chefTips: Semolina adds texture but don’t overuse – it can toughen the dough.
Keep dough covered to avoid drying.
Do not stack rolled puris – they may stick.
Dough might be too soft or rolled unevenly.
Best served hot with potato curry (aloo bhaji), chana masala, or halwa.
